data.reasoning:
BUY 865 NVDA (~$89,960) — Thesis: Long conviction position betting on sustained AI infrastructure demand beyond near-term consensus. Thesis rests on three pillars: (1) expanding production capacity signals management confidence in demand durability; (2) multi-customer backlog reduces single-customer dependency risk (e.g., over-reliance on hyperscalers); (3) management guidance for stronger-than-expected orders and capacity requirements suggests AI spending extends beyond cloud providers into broadening enterprise segment. This supports a more structural, less cyclical view of the AI infrastructure cycle.

Key Risks: (1) Demand normalization — if enterprise AI spending inflects downward or capex cycles compress, the backlog visibility advantage evaporates quickly; (2) Competitive erosion — alternative accelerators (AMD, custom silicon from hyperscalers, open standards) could gain meaningful share and compress NVIDIA's pricing power; (3) Execution risk on new capacity ramps, which could delay revenue realization or introduce yield/cost issues; (4) Margin compression if supply normalizes faster than demand, forcing NVIDIA to defend share through pricing. Position size (~$90k) is material and concentrates semiconductor cycle risk. Committee sign-off required to confirm thesis durability and risk appetite for execution and competitive risks.
